judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all five expectations with proper file citations; Report 1 surfaces additional findings (unsupported rerouting claim, process-flow ADR) while Report 2 more concisely ties contradictions together (e.g., noting the draft's own rollback line contradicts its rerouting claim) — overall comparable quality with a slight edge to Report 1 for breadth.

report_1.evidence: Correct "Our monitoring detected the issue within minutes and our team responded immediately." `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md` shows failures began at 14:03 and the first alert fired at 14:50 — a 47-minute detection gap

report_2.evidence: The timeline shows failures began at 14:03 and the first alert fired at 14:50 — 47 minutes later. ... Evidence: `incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md` vs. draft lines 6–7.

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md` records that 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing, and 37 were unrecoverable and refunded.

report_2.evidence: The internal timeline (`incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md`) records that 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing, and 37 were unrecoverable and refunded.

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md` states the contractual monthly SLA is 99.9% and any month below it is a breach owing service credits scaled to the depth of the breach. `larkpay-comms/metrics/uptime-2026.csv` shows April 2026 at 99.77% — a breach.

report_2.evidence: April 2026 uptime was 99.77% (`metrics/uptime-2026.csv`), below the 99.9% contractual monthly SLA in `docs/sla-commitments.md`, which states any such month "is a breach and owes service credits."

report_1.evidence: Remove the blame on the on-call engineer.** "The outage was caused by the on-call engineer deploying an untested config change" violates rule 1 of `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` ("Never assign blame to an individual").

report_2.evidence: The draft blames a named individual role, violating style guide rule 1.** Draft lines 12–13 attribute the outage to "the on-call engineer deploying an untested config change." `docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 1: "Never assign blame to an individual."

report_1.evidence: Restructure to lead with customer impact and remediation.** The draft opens with a reputation defense and buries the (single-sentence) remediation at the end, violating rule 3 of `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md`.

report_2.evidence: The draft does not lead with customer impact and remediation, violating style guide rule 3.** It opens with an uptime boast (lines 3–4) and closes with more self-praise about engineering culture (lines 16–19). ... Per `docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 3, the structure must be inverted: impact and remediation first
